choice: Klebsiella pneumoniae first; S. aureus/MRSA documented follow-up
rationale: Depth over breadth per the challenge; rich BV-BRC lab-AST data and clear mechanisms.

choice: Self-source from BV-BRC; lab-measured AST only (evidence == 'Laboratory Method')
rationale: No organizer dataset; challenge warns against model-generated 'general phenotype' fields.

choice: AMRFinderPlus via pinned Docker image under WSL2
rationale: NCBI default; --organism Klebsiella_pneumoniae enables QRDR point mutations; Linux-native so Docker/WSL2.

choice: Per-antibiotic L2 logistic regression, class_weight='balanced' (classical ML is the star)
rationale: Fast, calibratable, explainable; per-drug models allow independent calibration/conformal/evidence.

choice: Sigmoid calibration (cv='prefit') + conformal prediction (crepes Mondrian primary, MAPIE cross-check)
rationale: Coverage-guaranteed abstention; sigmoid safer than isotonic for small per-drug folds.

choice: Homology-aware grouped split: MLST ST primary, Mash single-linkage @ ANI 99.5% fallback; StratifiedGroupKFold + unseen-lineage holdout
rationale: Prevents near-identical clonal genomes leaking across splits and inflating metrics.

choice: LLM surgical & structurally barred from prediction: evidence RAG, grounded report, LLM-as-reviewer; OpenAI primary, MockLLMClient in CI
rationale: Verdicts must be deterministic/auditable; LLM output schemas contain no verdict field; import-boundary CI check.

choice: Streamlit front + FastAPI backend; deploy to Streamlit Community Cloud
rationale: Challenge requires a Streamlit/Gradio demo; FastAPI gives a clean reusable API surface.

choice: Apache-2.0 + DISCLAIMER
rationale: Explicit patent grant, startup/acquisition-friendly for a patent-aware author; permissive for reuse.

choice: Full six-layer Sustainable Agentic SE framework as a live case study; documentation written before code
rationale: Ships fast while generating fresh ground-truth for the paper; no finding lives only in chat.

choice: arc42 as 12 chapter folders each with README.md (matching open-garden-planner); prd.md in chapter 01; verbatim challenge PDF as challenge-brief.md in chapter 01; all process flows as mermaid
rationale: Harness-hardening review by Sebastian — the initial six-layer setup must match the reference harness exactly before building on it.

choice: senior-reviewer agent (mandatory pre-PR gate) + skills (debug-verbose, finalize-epic, gf-build-and-run, gf-data-and-annotation); gitignore .claude/.mcp.json and Documentation/reuse-inventory.md
rationale: Replicate the open-garden-planner .claude harness; keep API keys (Tavily/OpenAI) and the private reuse map out of git.

choice: Draft-PR is the mandatory end state; PR stays draft with a falsifiable manual-testing checklist until the user explicitly confirms; fresh-worktree senior review re-run until no P0/P1; Plan Mode + alternative designs before any non-trivial change
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: automated green + review are necessary but not sufficient; manual testing is sovereign (repeatedly overturned reviewed/green work in the reference project).

choice: Single-source version at src/genome_firewall/__init__.py:__version__ (pyproject reads it via hatch dynamic); never create tags manually; release automation deferred with documented future trigger/bump policy
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: removes the two-file drift-bug class; nothing to release at EPIC-0, so document the deferral instead of building release.yml prematurely.

choice: Run CI on pushes to all branches AND on pull requests (concurrency cancels superseded runs)
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: feature branches get green/red feedback before the draft PR is opened; cancel-in-progress bounds cost.

choice: Add 8 gf-* process skills (change-control, validation-and-qa, docs-and-writing, architecture-contract, research-methodology, proof-and-analysis, failure-archaeology, pr-analysis) + a routing table in CLAUDE.md; adapt reference skills, do not copy Qt/CAD/garden/.ogp/i18n rules
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: give agents explicit routing for change control, evidence, docs, safety invariants, research, proof, historical failures, and PR analysis before EPIC 1 accelerates.

choice: Mandatory, no-exceptions capture of every non-obvious bug/lesson in the same session: canonical detailed log at Documentation/11-risks-and-technical-debt/ §11.4 + debug-verbose case study + CLAUDE.md Known-AI-pitfall line + pinning regression test; senior-reviewer treats a missing capture as P1
rationale: Match the reference project's strictness (must not be less strict than open-garden-planner); a lesson that lives only in chat is lost.

choice: Floor the ml extra with numba>=0.60 (skips ancient numba 0.53.1/llvmlite 0.36.0 that break on py3.11/3.12); add tests/test_smoke.py to satisfy the coverage gate honestly
rationale: Pre-existing dependency defect on main made uv sync --all-extras fail all CI jobs; the scaffold had zero tests so the 80% coverage gate could not pass. Both fixed without weakening any gate.

choice: Pure Python: ftplib.FTP_TLS for the FTPS flat file + per-genome .fna, stdlib urllib.request for the Solr Data API cross-check; no WSL2/p3-CLI dependency
rationale: Cross-platform, unit-testable, matches 'demo pure-Python, no bio-tools in CI'; avoids a heavy WSL2 toolchain for what is plain FTPS/HTTPS.

choice: Add pyarrow>=16 as a core dependency for pandas to_parquet/read_parquet
rationale: The PRD's Artifacts section already commits to Parquet for every processed table; pandas has no Parquet engine of its own, and EPIC 3 will be the first to read labels.parquet back.

choice: Trust-critical parse/filter/canonicalize/collapse-duplicate logic lives in predictor/dataset.py (pure, no network, mypy --strict, coverage-gated); all FTPS/Solr network I/O lives only in scripts/, outside those gates by design. Majority-vote SIR collapse on (genome_id, antibiotic); ties/conflicts dropped, never guessed at. 3-class SIR persisted (Intermediate kept); binary S-vs-R collapse deferred to EPIC 3.
rationale: Puts the one filter that most determines label integrity (evidence=='Laboratory Method', ADR-0001) under the strictest gates in the repo; keeps network flakiness out of the trust-critical path.

choice: Confirmed against the real Solr API (taxon_id=573): evidence has exactly 3 values in production -- 'Computational Method' (1,728,894 rows), 'Laboratory Method' (85,291 rows, exact match to the research doc), 'Computational Prediction' (0 rows). The evidence=='Laboratory Method' filter is correctly scoped -- no lab data hides under a different label.
rationale: Issue #12 explicitly requires enumerating all distinct evidence values before finalizing the filter, not assuming the research doc's sandboxed estimate; running it live closes that to-validate item with a hard number.

choice: feature_matrix.parquet (needs AMRFinderPlus) deferred to EPIC 2/#17; train/test splits (ADR-0005 homology-aware MLST+Mash grouping) deferred to EPIC 3/#18. labels.parquet ships split-agnostic; genome_metadata.parquet's nullable mlst_st + the manifest's mlst.missing_fraction give #18 the Mash-fallback scope up front.
rationale: Keeps EPIC 1 to data ingestion only, matching the module dependency graph (annotation and splitting both require inputs EPIC 1 produces); carry-forward comments posted on #11/#13/#17/#18 so nothing is silently dropped.

choice: Feature builder lives in reader/feature_builder.py (not a separate features/ package); features/ stays reserved for EPIC 3's per-drug ML feature engineering built on top of GenomeFeatureVector. AMRFinderPlus Docker runner + MockAnnotator live in annotation/, matching the existing stub and golden rule #6. One feature branch/PR for all of EPIC 2 (#3/#14/#15/#16/#17), matching the EPIC 1 precedent.
rationale: Resolves a PRD-vs-arc42 wording mismatch (PRD bundled 'feature builder' under reader/; building-block-view implied a separate features/ package) in favor of keeping all of Module 01 (parse->annotate->build) in one package; the four EPIC 2 pieces are tightly coupled enough that one PR beats four.

choice: Define the full cross-epic Pydantic contract now -- GenomeInput, ContigRecord, AmrFeature, AnnotationResult, GenomeFeatureVector, GateResult, ModelPrediction, ConformalSet, EvidenceItem, AntibioticPrediction, GenomeReport -- even though EPIC 2's own code only exercises the reader-side models. The no-call contract (conformal set -> verdict) and the disclaimer-exact-match rule are enforced as Pydantic cross-field validators, pinned by tests/test_schemas.py despite not yet being exercised by any pipeline code.
rationale: Matches research-findings/architecture.md's build order (schemas first, everything downstream imports from it) and issue #14's literal field list; lets EPIC 3/4 build on a stable contract instead of touching schemas.py again mid-epic. The safety-critical validators are P0 per gf-architecture-contract, so they are tested directly rather than left untested until a later epic happens to exercise them.

choice: Commit a pinned copy at data/reference/ReferenceGeneCatalog.txt (2.3MB, DB version 2026-05-15.1) rather than fetching it at build/run time. Its allele column matches AMRFinderPlus's Element symbol directly for named alleles/point mutations; gene_family is the fallback key for genes without allele-level naming. STRESS-type genes have blank Class/Subclass in the catalog itself (not a data-quality gap) so the fallback only applies to Element subtype==AMR rows.
rationale: Small enough to commit like existing fixture data; removes a network dependency from feature-building. Confirmed reachable and inspected directly (not assumed from the research doc) before committing to the join-key design.

decision: Real AMRFinderPlus validation run + AmrMethod correction (issue #16)
choice: Ran the pinned ncbi/amr:4.2.7-2026-05-15.1 Docker image against a real NCBI RefSeq K. pneumoniae genome (GCF_000016305.1, MGH 78578) during implementation rather than deferring all real-tool validation to the user's manual test. This surfaced that AMRFinderPlus's real Method values are X/P/N-suffixed (ALLELEX, EXACTX, BLASTX, PARTIALX, PARTIAL_CONTIG_ENDX, POINTX/POINTP/POINTN) and its TSV header uses Type/Subtype (not the paraphrased 'Element type'/'Element subtype') -- schemas.py:AmrMethod was corrected before any fixture was built from it.
rationale: ADR-0002 flagged mock-vs-real drift as a to-validate risk; running the real tool once, early, converted that into a closed validation rather than a deferred hope. Docker/network access was confirmed available in-session, so there was no reason to make the user source this.

decision: Near-duplicate gene hits are counted, not collapsed
choice: Added GenomeFeatureVector.gene_hit_count (per-gene count of matching AMRFinderPlus rows) alongside gene_presence/gene_presence_method. A gene hit twice at different loci (observed for real: blaTEM-1 in the MGH 78578 validation genome) is recorded as count=2, not silently collapsed to a single presence=True.
rationale: research-findings/amrfinderplus-features.md explicitly warns against silently deduplicating near-duplicate/multi-contig hits for the same gene; a real genome in this session's own validation run happened to demonstrate exactly that case.

choice: Make the HTTPS Data API the primary fetch path (genome_amr lab-AST rows + genome_sequence contig FASTA via application/dna+fasta) and demote FTPS to a documented, still-tested fallback. Validated live this session against the real API. Added a mandatory FASTA sanity check (contig count/length vs the genome record) to catch the Data API's silent default-limit truncation.
rationale: The user chose 'investigate the non-FTPS path first' because FTPS hits a router-ALG 425 failure on the dev network (Known Pitfall). A fully-HTTPS route exists and works, removing the fragile FTPS dependency from the real run.

choice: MLST ST primary grouping; a genome without a usable ST becomes its own singleton group (leakage-safe, needs no external tool). Clustering is behind a pluggable ClusterBackend; real Mash/skani ANI-99.5% clustering is deferred with an AniClusterBackend NotImplementedError stub.
rationale: Neither Mash nor skani is installed and an external ANI tool could never run in CI. The singleton fallback preserves the no-leakage guarantee (a singleton can appear on only one side of any split boundary) while keeping tests tool-free; the pluggable backend makes adopting ANI later a drop-in.

choice: Map only Resistant->R and Susceptible->S; drop Intermediate, Nonsusceptible, and Susceptible-dose dependent as ambiguous label-noise rather than force-mapping them. Enforced by an import-time partition guard over constants.BINARY_*/DROPPED_SIR_CLASSES.
rationale: Lowest label noise and the defensive choice; the user selected 'drop both rare classes' over mapping Nonsusceptible->R. Revisit per-drug if a drug's label volume is thin after the grouped split.

choice: The target gate only ever forces likely_to_fail on a called known resistance mechanism; it never forces likely_to_work. Susceptibility is concluded only by the calibrated model + conformal (which can no-call). target_present is recorded (True for all panel drugs). Per-drug firing uses AMRFinderPlus Subclass for beta-lactam tiers and combination thresholds for fluoroquinolones/aminoglycosides.
rationale: The challenge brief requires the system not to report 'likely to work' from marker-absence (carbapenemase-negative != susceptible, porin-loss route). The user deferred to 'the most scientifically viable route'; one-directional is the defensible reading and structurally prevents confident-but-wrong 'work' calls.

choice: Rely on LogisticRegression's default L2 penalty instead of passing penalty='l2' (deprecated in sklearn 1.8), and express cv='prefit' sigmoid calibration by wrapping the base model in sklearn.frozen.FrozenEstimator (cv='prefit' removed by sklearn 1.8; env runs 1.9). Same statistical method, adapted call.
rationale: ADR-0003/0004 were written against an older sklearn API; the resolved dependency (1.9) deprecated/removed those exact calls. Adapting deliberately (and recording it) keeps the ADR method intact without FutureWarnings or a removed-API break.

decision: Combined EPIC 4+5 work package forked off main, decoupled from in-flight predict.py
choice: Build the deterministic report builder (EPIC 4) and the RAG+LLM narrative (EPIC 5) as one branch off main. The builder consumes primitive predictor-output schemas already on main (GateResult/ModelPrediction/ConformalSet via report/inputs.DrugPredictionInput) and re-runs the pure evaluate_gate, never importing the unbuilt predictor.predict; narrator/reviewer live in report/, not a new agents/ package.
rationale: The schema contract (schemas.py) is the only cross-epic dependency and is already on main; the import-boundary makes report/llm/kb strictly downstream of the predictor, so this develops in parallel with the EPIC 3 predict-registry session without file conflicts. report/ as the home for the narrative sub-pipeline matches the arc42 building-block view (05).

decision: Evidence RAG dense leg is offline-safe behind an Embedder Protocol
choice: Hybrid retrieval = BM25 (always on) + optional dense leg fused by RRF (RRF_K=60). CI/tests inject a deterministic HashingBagOfWordsEmbedder (numpy-only, BLAKE2b hashing); the real SentenceTransformerEmbedder is constructed lazily and never in CI. The 2.3MB ReferenceGeneCatalog is never read in CI; a small curated seed corpus is committed.
rationale: sentence-transformers downloads model weights on first construction, which would fail the offline no-network CI. Isolating the dense leg behind an interface (the AMRFinderPlus pattern) exercises the full hybrid+RRF path deterministically offline while the real model swaps in for prod/manual use. Stated MVP limitation: the CI dense leg is lexical, not learned embeddings.

decision: Evidence-category tagging and fail-closed narrative envelope
choice: Per-item evidence_category is set by curated-KB membership (features/mechanisms.py); the row category is the strongest cited item (known_mechanism > statistical_association > no_signal). Category and confidence are independent (gate hit -> 0.99; else calibrated model value). The LLM review outcome is returned on a NarrativeEnvelope (review_status/source/grounding/error) rather than mutating the frozen GenomeReport; a deterministic pre-check runs before the LLM judge; disable/error/rejection fall back to the deterministic template.
rationale: A gate-anchored row category trips AntibioticPrediction validator #3 for a lone KB-member gene (e.g. a single qnrB); deriving the summary from its cited items (a max/rollup) satisfies all validators by construction and is more honest. GenomeReport is frozen, so the envelope keeps review status machine-readable without a schema change. The deterministic pre-check + fail-closed makes the reviewer load-bearing at zero extra cost (the template must exist as the no-key fallback anyway).

choice: Port a slimmed, crash-safe MLflowTracker + NullTracker into predictor/experiment_tracking.py, tracking each per-drug run to a local ./mlruns file store; strictly off the inference path (predict.py never imports it; the NullTracker default yields byte-identical models). Dropped all Qt/GUI/deep-link/UI-server code from the reference digitalsreeni-image-annotator implementation.
rationale: The tracking extra (mlflow>=2.14), ./mlruns gitignore, and the mlflow.* mypy override already existed from EPIC 0, so this is a proven-component port + ADR, not a new dependency. Crash-safety guarantees a tracking failure never aborts training or touches a verdict.

decision: Conformal inference is pure-numpy; per-genome evidence is closed-form linear SHAP
choice: Ship class-conditional (Mondrian) LAC split-conformal as two numeric thresholds so inference needs no crepes/MAPIE (kept only as train-time coverage cross-checks). Per-genome statistical evidence cites the L2-LR signed coefficient of each PRESENT binary feature, which for a 0/1 feature against an all-absent reference is the exact linear Shapley value -- so no shap/numba runtime is pulled onto the verdict path.
rationale: Keeps the shipped inference dependency-light and trivially testable (the same 'implement the guarantee directly, validate empirically' adaptation ADR-0004 anticipated); the linear-SHAP equivalence is exact, not an approximation.

decision: Filesystem model registry with recorded insufficient-data status
choice: Persist each trained drug to models/<slug>/v<N>/ (calibrated_model.joblib gitignored; feature_schema.json/conformal.json/coefficients.json/metrics.json/model_card.md committed as ground-truth); registry.json records per-drug status (trained|insufficient_data) + the base annotation/schema versions. predict.py fails loud with a typed error (AmrfinderDbVersionMismatchError/FeatureSchemaMismatchError) on a version mismatch, and distinguishes a recorded insufficient-data no_call from a missing-model error.
rationale: A silent version/schema mismatch would attach a model's coefficients to the wrong genes and corrupt every verdict (golden rule #3); the recorded status lets predict.py give an honest no_call for a thin drug instead of erroring.

decision: BV-BRC live mlst field carries an 'MLST.' prefix (real-data fix)
choice: parse_mlst strips a leading 'MLST.' before matching the scheme.ST pattern. On the live HTTPS API 36,885/39,628 K. pneumoniae genomes then recover a real ST (vs 0 before the fix), and the 120-genome subset spans 67 STs instead of 120 singletons -- restoring the homology-aware split's whole purpose.
rationale: The EPIC 1 fixtures used the bare 'klebsiella.258' form; the live Data API prefixes 'MLST.', which the regex never matched, silently degrading the grouped split to a random per-genome split. Same class as the AMRFinderPlus Method-suffix and genome_sequence page-cap lessons -- verify parsers against live output, not fixtures.

choice: Trained all 5 per-drug models on a 130-genome/67-ST real BV-BRC subset. Honest gate-negative headline metrics: gentamicin (the designed model-drug, AME-driven) AUROC 0.875 / PR-AUC 0.76 / resistant-recall 0.5; the beta-lactams' gate-negative residual is too thin to learn (the one-directional gate correctly removes the carbapenemase/ESBL signal, leaving near-zero learnable R), surfaced honestly via conformal_guarantee_available=false (4/5 drugs) and high no-call rates rather than inflated accuracy. Small-n limitation recorded, not hidden.
rationale: Decision #9 reports metrics on the gate-negative population the model actually serves; at n=130 that residual is thin for the gate-dominated beta-lactams. The result validates the pipeline end-to-end and the gentamicin model, and the reporting design makes the thin-data drugs self-evident.

decision: Guarantee-void conformal verdicts are caveated, not downgraded
choice: When a per-drug conformal calibration set is below the finite-sample floor (guarantee_available=false), predict.py surfaces an explicit statistical_association caveat on every affected verdict (supporting_features + a cited EvidenceItem) but does NOT downgrade the work/fail verdict to no_call.
rationale: guarantee_available reflects calibration-set SIZE, not model quality: gentamicin is guarantee-void yet AUROC 0.875, so a blanket downgrade would discard the one strong model. Surfacing the caveat is the honest, non-lossy choice; the Module 03 report layer must render the caveat as prominently as the numeric calibrated_confidence (recorded as forward debt in 11.4).

decision: Default LLM = GPT-5.6 Luna at Extra-High reasoning (temperature omitted)
choice: Default OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5.6-luna and OPENAI_REASONING_EFFORT=xhigh; the backend sends reasoning_effort and omits temperature (both optional, sent only when set). Verified live: gpt-5.6-luna exists; reasoning_effort accepts none|low|medium|high|xhigh (xhigh=Extra High); the model rejects temperature other than default(1). Full narrate_report smoke-tested live at low (accepted, grounding 1.0) and xhigh (one run accepted grounding 1.0, one run reviewer-rejected then fail-closed to template).
rationale: User-directed model choice. Reasoning models change the request shape vs gpt-4o (require reasoning_effort, reject temperature); verified against the live API before adoption rather than assumed. CI stays mock-only; the deterministic template remains the safe floor when a nondeterministic run is rejected.
